title: Error when trying to submit a form (LLM retry loop returns on first attempt)
one_liner: Reported that the retry `for _ in range(10)` loop returned on the first iteration; current llm.py has been rewritten with a proper attempt loop and exception handling, so this is already fixed.

title: Cloudflare Protection Blocking Crunchbase Results
one_liner: Scraping Crunchbase via Tavily hits Cloudflare blocks; suggested adding Tavily Extract / headless-browser fallback to bypass anti-bot pages, which is a real but non-trivial scraper enhancement.

title: Error When Running Ollama LLM Test Script for GPTR (OLLAMA_BASE_URL KeyError)
one_liner: Docs Ollama test script passes base_url both as kwarg and via os.environ['OLLAMA_BASE_URL'], causing KeyError / duplicate-arg errors; fix is to align the doc script and base.py to read OLLAMA_BASE_URL from env safely.

title: o3 / reasoning models reject temperature argument
one_liner: Requested support for o3/o-series models that don't accept temperature; base.py now has NO_SUPPORT_TEMPERATURE_MODELS handling for o1/o3/o4/gpt-5, so the core ask is implemented (LiteLLM users must match exact model name) and the thread can be closed.

title: Security Issue - LLM05 - Insecure output handling (XSS)
one_liner: Reports containing XSS payloads render unsanitized in the hosted/vanilla frontend; needs markdown sanitization on report rendering (NextJS frontend likely safer). Real concern but scope/owner decision needed.

title: Inconsistent required Python versions
one_liner: pyproject said python >=3.10,<3.12 while README said 3.11+; pyproject.toml now declares python >=3.11 (and requires-python >=3.11), so the inconsistency is resolved.

title: Proposal: Unified Provider Configuration Management
one_liner: Proposes type-safe/dataclass-based unified provider (esp. Azure OpenAI) config instead of scattered env vars + JSON; a sizable architectural change requiring maintainer direction.

title: Detailed Report on local docs: missing/empty references and broken citations
one_liner: Detailed reports over local docs produce empty reference lists and hallucinated URLs because local documents never get URLs set in visited_urls; fix is to populate document file paths/URLs so add_references works.

title: Long prompts give FileNotFoundError (filename too long)
one_liner: Long queries created over-long output filenames on Windows; sanitize_filename in server_utils.py now hashes the task portion (md5[:10]) instead of embedding the full prompt, so this is fixed.

title: Multi-Agent endless loop / endless failure in json_repair
one_liner: Multi-agent reviewer loop can spin forever when LLM JSON can't be repaired (often truncated by low SMART_TOKEN_LIMIT); needs a max-retry / hard-failure guard and a shared text->JSON util in multi_agents/agents/utils/llms.py.

title: Multi-Agent reviewer following guidelines makes output inconsistent (missing headers/refs)
one_liner: Multi-agent reports show missing markdown header formatting and inconsistent reference aggregation, largely prompt/model-quality dependent (non-OpenAI models); needs prompt hardening rather than a clear single fix.

title: openai.BadRequestError using openai:o1-preview and Deep Research (developer role)
one_liner: o1-preview rejects the 'developer' role / reasoning params; o1-preview is now in NO_SUPPORT_TEMPERATURE_MODELS and maintainers recommended o1/o3-mini. Likely resolved for supported models; close or verify developer-role handling.

title: Lightweight docker image (without Playwright)
one_liner: Requests a slim Docker image (~680MB) without Playwright for users relying on external scrapers like Firecrawl; reporter offered a PR, so maintainer just needs to greenlight an additional lite Dockerfile.

title: Feature request: use official tavily python client (async)
one_liner: Asks why GPTR uses raw HTTP instead of tavily-python's Async client; maintainer explained intentional choice to keep the pip package light. Answer and close (or label as low-priority enhancement).

title: gpt-researcher npm package does not support https/wss host
one_liner: npm package hardcoded ws:// instead of wss:// for https hosts; a contributor commented that http/https support has since been added to the npm package, so verify and close.

title: Chapters on reports generated by multi-agent systems don't match TOC
one_liner: Chapter names vs. appended chapter list mismatch when using a local Ollama 32b model; maintainer indicated this is model-quality related (better on OpenAI). Likely a duplicate of the multi-agent formatting issues; close or merge into #1139.

title: Issue with Anthropic llm provider (claude-2.1 / opus)
one_liner: Error in get_llm when configuring anthropic provider with old models (claude-2.1, claude-3-opus); truncated traceback. Anthropic support exists in base.py, so ask for full error and suggest current model names / installing langchain_anthropic.

title: Parse Dimensions Function Appears to Be Incorrect
one_liner: parse_dimension converted to int and didn't handle decimals/%; current scraper/utils.py now does int(float(value)) handling decimals. Percent handling still absent but the core bug is fixed; close or label-only.

title: Validate followup chat feature for newly-created & saved reports
one_liner: Follow-up chat on saved reports throws WebSocket close-code 1005; needs a new API endpoint to chat with previously saved reports (referenced PR #1241). Feature work requiring maintainer scoping.

title: Feature Request: Adding Human Feedback to Research Strategy Planning
one_liner: Wants a human-in-the-loop review/edit step before research strategy is finalized; maintainer notes this already exists for the multi_agents flow via include_human_feedback in task.json. Point user there and close, or keep open for the single-agent flow.

title: Fails at fairly straightforward, deterministic search tasks
one_liner: User expects exhaustive deterministic results (enumerating files across a repo) that GPTR doesn't fully return; this is a general capability expectation. Maintainer already asked for the config/report type; await response or close as out-of-scope.

title: Long queries cause an uncaught Tavily bug (400, max 400 chars)
one_liner: Tavily rejects queries >400 chars with a 400 error that propagates uncaught; tavily_search.py has no length guard. Fix is to truncate/validate query length before calling the Tavily API.

title: Never Ending Generation Loop with Ollama and Searxng
one_liner: With Ollama models the report generation never terminates (repeats 'End of Document'/References endlessly); appears to be a missing stop-token/length guard for non-OpenAI models. Reproducible across many models, but root cause needs investigation.

title: No error handling on empty search results from retriever
one_liner: When the retriever returns zero results, empty context is streamed to the LLM producing an unhelpful response; add explicit handling/logging and a friendly 'no sources found' message instead of generating a report.

title: Are there any known issues with the reference section?
one_liner: References frequently have wrong author/link, duplicate links, or 404s (LLM mismatches source URLs during synthesis); affects multiple users/models. Needs improved citation grounding (map references to actual visited_urls) but root cause is fuzzy.

title: AttributeError: 'list' object has no attribute 'split' in deep research (count_words)
one_liner: count_words crashed on list input when CURATE_SOURCES=true; deep_research.py count_words now handles lists and there's an explicit extend-vs-append fix for curated context. Likely resolved on latest master though one comment (2026-04) reports it on an older pip; verify version and close.
